---
schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: 37dfaaaafacd8f42c6b6c42aaa9e3a0eeac672ee
findings:
  - { id: judged-illustrative-marker-derived-vs-ratified, kind: judged, text: "Story dc-2 makes the illustrative tier a derived pure function of the artifact bytes (illustrative BY LOCATION or BY CLASS — 'no new field'), but parent dc-7 ratifies 'the illustrative marker' as part of the one 02 schema-amendment batch that must land before implementation. If the tier is fully derivable with no declared field, the story silently contradicts (or obviates) a schema surface the parent decided to ratify; no supersedes/exempts edge is declared against dc-7. (confidence 0.60)", disposition: no-conflict, note: "the ratified 02 batch itself defines what 'the illustrative marker' IS, and it is the location convention, not a schema field: 02 §Diagram proposals, 'The illustrative marker' — 'it is a body figure, not a first-class artifact: a fenced mermaid block (or a diagrams/ file reference) in a spec's own body prose ... always badged deterministically-unverifiable ... The two coverage tiers become two document locations.' Story dc-2's by-location/by-class rule is that ratified marker restated, and 'no new field' agrees with, rather than obviates, what dc-7's batch actually froze into 02.", target_ref: spec/diagram-proposals }
  - { id: judged-reference-form-link-vs-body-figure, kind: judged, text: "Parent dc-8 defines the illustrative attachment convention as a body figure realized as EITHER a fenced mermaid block OR a diagrams/ reference in the spec body, 'already rendered by the dex.' Story dc-3 demotes the reference form to a bare link to the artifact's own page with no inline figure or badge in the owning body — so one of the parent's two sanctioned body-figure forms no longer attaches as a body figure at all. No supersedes/exempts edge is declared for this narrowing of dc-8. (confidence 0.55)", disposition: no-conflict, note: "dc-8's 'already rendered by the dex' is a claim about existing behavior, and the existing behavior is exactly what dc-3 keeps: the dex has no transclusion path anywhere (internal/render renders fenced blocks and diagram-kind artifact bodies; a diagrams/ reference in body prose renders as a link today), so the phrase can only truthfully describe the fenced path plus the target artifact's own page. dc-3 narrows nothing that exists: both attachment forms remain attachable, and the badge is disclosed at each form's actual render site — inline for the fenced figure, on the target page for the reference. Inline transclusion would be NEW behavior, which dc-3 records as deliberately deferred (smallest reversible), layerable later without changing the badge grammar.", target_ref: spec/diagram-proposals }
  - { id: judged-tier-pure-function-vs-fail-closed-extraction, kind: judged, text: "Story dc-2 asserts the badge tier is decidable 'without the extractor... a pure function of the artifact bytes,' but parent dc-1 establishes that extraction failure fails closed to a lower verification tier — meaning a class: proposal artifact's effective tier (potentially down to illustrative) can depend on extractor outcome, not bytes-only classification. The story's scoping ('full/partial stays with the verification-extractor story') does not declare how a fail-closed proposal interacts with the byte-pure illustrative badge, leaving an undeclared boundary conflict with dc-1. (confidence 0.45)", disposition: no-conflict, note: "the boundary is declared, in dc-2 and ac-3 together: for the two non-proposal locations the badge is byte-pure (by location, by class — no extractor can even apply, so purity is total); for a class: proposal, this story never computes any tier — the proposal's surfaces render the EXTRACTOR-computed tier (ac-3), and if parent dc-1's fail-closed rule lowers that computed tier all the way to illustrative, that extractor-computed vocabulary is what renders, honestly, with its extractor provenance. What ac-2's negative forbids is only this story's byte-pure classifier painting a proposal — the two disclosures have different provenance and never collide, which is dc-1's fail-closed rule consumed as ratified, not contradicted.", target_ref: spec/diagram-proposals }
sweep_provenance: { adr_corpus_digest: sha256:37517e5f3dc66819f61f5a7bb8ace1921282415f10551d2defa5c3eb0985b570, decisions_scanned: [spec/diagram-proposals#dc-1, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-2, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-3, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-4, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-5, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-6, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-7, spec/diagram-proposals#dc-8, spec/illustrative-class#dc-1, spec/illustrative-class#dc-2, spec/illustrative-class#dc-3, spec/illustrative-class#dc-4] }
digest: sha256:88dc05882aa014ae7ca9cd5e8ba31521c508d72ad83557d00429abbf9f467890
integrity: sha256:36dd1669eda1e88de31ef8374a7832cb2a61cc2f4817310dfa6076d2877536e0
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"illustrative-marker-derived-vs-ratified\",\"text\":\"Story dc-2 makes the illustrative tier a derived pure function of the artifact bytes (illustrative BY LOCATION or BY CLASS — 'no new field'), but parent dc-7 ratifies 'the illustrative marker' as part of the one 02 schema-amendment batch that must land before implementation. If the tier is fully derivable with no declared field, the story silently contradicts (or obviates) a schema surface the parent decided to ratify; no supersedes/exempts edge is declared against dc-7.\",\"confidence\":0.6,\"target\":\"spec/diagram-proposals\"},{\"id\":\"reference-form-link-vs-body-figure\",\"text\":\"Parent dc-8 defines the illustrative attachment convention as a body figure realized as EITHER a fenced mermaid block OR a diagrams/ reference in the spec body, 'already rendered by the dex.' Story dc-3 demotes the reference form to a bare link to the artifact's own page with no inline figure or badge in the owning body — so one of the parent's two sanctioned body-figure forms no longer attaches as a body figure at all. No supersedes/exempts edge is declared for this narrowing of dc-8.\",\"confidence\":0.55,\"target\":\"spec/diagram-proposals\"},{\"id\":\"tier-pure-function-vs-fail-closed-extraction\",\"text\":\"Story dc-2 asserts the badge tier is decidable 'without the extractor... a pure function of the artifact bytes,' but parent dc-1 establishes that extraction failure fails closed to a lower verification tier — meaning a class: proposal artifact's effective tier (potentially down to illustrative) can depend on extractor outcome, not bytes-only classification. The story's scoping ('full/partial stays with the verification-extractor story') does not declare how a fail-closed proposal interacts with the byte-pure illustrative badge, leaving an undeclared boundary conflict with dc-1.\",\"confidence\":0.45,\"target\":\"spec/diagram-proposals\"}]}" }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v1, inputs: [spec/illustrative-class@37dfaaaafacd8f42c6b6c42aaa9e3a0eeac672ee], digest: sha256:88dc05882aa014ae7ca9cd5e8ba31521c508d72ad83557d00429abbf9f467890, integrity: sha256:36dd1669eda1e88de31ef8374a7832cb2a61cc2f4817310dfa6076d2877536e0 }
---
# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-illustrative-marker-derived-vs-ratified** [no-conflict]: Story dc-2 makes the illustrative tier a derived pure function of the artifact bytes (illustrative BY LOCATION or BY CLASS — 'no new field'), but parent dc-7 ratifies 'the illustrative marker' as part of the one 02 schema-amendment batch that must land before implementation. If the tier is fully derivable with no declared field, the story silently contradicts (or obviates) a schema surface the parent decided to ratify; no supersedes/exempts edge is declared against dc-7. (confidence 0.60) — the ratified 02 batch itself defines what 'the illustrative marker' IS, and it is the location convention, not a schema field: 02 §Diagram proposals, 'The illustrative marker' — 'it is a body figure, not a first-class artifact: a fenced mermaid block (or a diagrams/ file reference) in a spec's own body prose ... always badged deterministically-unverifiable ... The two coverage tiers become two document locations.' Story dc-2's by-location/by-class rule is that ratified marker restated, and 'no new field' agrees with, rather than obviates, what dc-7's batch actually froze into 02.
- **judged-reference-form-link-vs-body-figure** [no-conflict]: Parent dc-8 defines the illustrative attachment convention as a body figure realized as EITHER a fenced mermaid block OR a diagrams/ reference in the spec body, 'already rendered by the dex.' Story dc-3 demotes the reference form to a bare link to the artifact's own page with no inline figure or badge in the owning body — so one of the parent's two sanctioned body-figure forms no longer attaches as a body figure at all. No supersedes/exempts edge is declared for this narrowing of dc-8. (confidence 0.55) — dc-8's 'already rendered by the dex' is a claim about existing behavior, and the existing behavior is exactly what dc-3 keeps: the dex has no transclusion path anywhere (internal/render renders fenced blocks and diagram-kind artifact bodies; a diagrams/ reference in body prose renders as a link today), so the phrase can only truthfully describe the fenced path plus the target artifact's own page. dc-3 narrows nothing that exists: both attachment forms remain attachable, and the badge is disclosed at each form's actual render site — inline for the fenced figure, on the target page for the reference. Inline transclusion would be NEW behavior, which dc-3 records as deliberately deferred (smallest reversible), layerable later without changing the badge grammar.
- **judged-tier-pure-function-vs-fail-closed-extraction** [no-conflict]: Story dc-2 asserts the badge tier is decidable 'without the extractor... a pure function of the artifact bytes,' but parent dc-1 establishes that extraction failure fails closed to a lower verification tier — meaning a class: proposal artifact's effective tier (potentially down to illustrative) can depend on extractor outcome, not bytes-only classification. The story's scoping ('full/partial stays with the verification-extractor story') does not declare how a fail-closed proposal interacts with the byte-pure illustrative badge, leaving an undeclared boundary conflict with dc-1. (confidence 0.45) — the boundary is declared, in dc-2 and ac-3 together: for the two non-proposal locations the badge is byte-pure (by location, by class — no extractor can even apply, so purity is total); for a class: proposal, this story never computes any tier — the proposal's surfaces render the EXTRACTOR-computed tier (ac-3), and if parent dc-1's fail-closed rule lowers that computed tier all the way to illustrative, that extractor-computed vocabulary is what renders, honestly, with its extractor provenance. What ac-2's negative forbids is only this story's byte-pure classifier painting a proposal — the two disclosures have different provenance and never collide, which is dc-1's fail-closed rule consumed as ratified, not contradicted.
